info.title: Wazuh
info.description: Initialize with CURL request towards: https://10.0.3.141:55000/security/user/authenticate?raw=true with basic auth using the HTTP app. The Wazuh API is an open source RESTful API that allows for interaction with the Wazuh manager from a web browser,command line tool like cURL or any script or program that can make web requests. The Wazuh WUI relies on thisheavily and Wazuhs goal is to accommodate complete remote management of the Wazuh infrastructure via the Wazuh WUI.Use the Wazuh API to easily perform everyday actions like adding an agent, restarting the manager(s) or agent(s)or looking up syscheck details.# AuthenticationWazuh API endpoints require authentication in order to be used. Therefore, all calls must include a JSON Web Token.JWT is an open standard (RFC 7519) that defines a compact and self-contained way for securely transmittinginformation between parties as a JSON object. Perform a call with `basicAuth` to `GET /security/user/authenticate`and obtain a JWT token in order to run any endpoint.JWT tokens have a default duration of 900 seconds. To change this value, you must perform a call with a validJWT token to `PUT /security/config`. After this change, you will need to get a new JWT token as all previouslyissued tokens are revoked when any change is performed on security configuration.Login with USER and PASSWORD:`curl -u <USER>:<PASSWORD> -k -X GET "https://<HOST_IP>:55000/security/user/authenticate"````json{    "data": {        "token": "<YOUR_JWT_TOKEN>"    },    "error": 0}```Use the token from previous response to perform any endpoint request:`curl -k -X <METHOD> "https://<HOST_IP>:55000/<ENDPOINT>" -H  "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_JWT_TOKEN>"`Change the token base duration:`curl -k -X PUT "https://<HOST_IP>:55000/security/config" -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_JWT_TOKEN>"-d '{"auth_token_exp_timeout":<NEW_EXPIRE_TIME_IN_SECONDS>}'`<SecurityDefinitions />

schema.example:
{
  "api_version": "API version in the manager",
  "hostname": "Server hostname",
  "license_name": "API license name",
  "license_url": "API license url",
  "revision": 0,
  "timestamp": "",
  "title": "API title name"
}

get.description: Return basic information about the API

put.description: Run an Active Response command on all agents or a list of them

parameters.name: agents_list
parameters.description: List of agent IDs (separated by comma), all agents selected by default if not specified

get.description: Return the rootcheck database of an agent

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: First element to return in the collection

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to return. Although up to 100.000 can be specified, it is recommended not to exceed 500 elements. Responses may be slower the more this number is exceeded.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort the collection by a field or fields (separated by comma). Use +/- at the beggining to list in ascending or descending order. Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Look for elements containing the specified string. To obtain a complementary search, use '-' at the beggining

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Select which fields to return (separated by comma). Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query to filter results by. For example q="status=active"

delete.description: Delete all agents or a list of them based on optional criteria

parameters.name: agents_list
parameters.description: List of agent IDs (separated by comma), use the keyword `all` to select all agents

parameters.name: purge
parameters.description: Permanently delete an agent from the key store

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by agent status (use commas to enter multiple statuses)

parameters.name: older_than
parameters.description: Consider only agents whose last keep alive is older than the specified time frame. For never_connected agents, register date is considered instead of last keep alive. For example, `7d`, `10s` and `10` are valid values. When no time unit is specified, seconds are assumed. Use 0s to select all agents

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query to filter results by. For example q="status=active"

parameters.name: manager
parameters.description: Filter by manager hostname where agents are connected to

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: Filter by the IP used by the agent to communicate with the manager. If it's not available, it will have the same value as registerIP

parameters.name: registerIP
parameters.description: Filter by the IP used when registering the agent

parameters.name: older_than
parameters.description: Filter out agents whose time lapse from last keep alive signal is longer than specified. Time in seconds, [n_days]d, [n_hours]h, [n_minutes]m or [n_seconds]s. For never_connected agents, uses the register date. For example, `7d`, `10s` and `10` are valid values. If no time unit is specified, seconds are used

parameters.name: agents_list
parameters.description: List of agent IDs (separated by comma), select a list of agents with size less or equal than 100

parameters.name: use_http
parameters.description: Use http protocol. If it's false use https. By default the value is set to false

put.description: Upgrade the agents using a local WPK file

parameters.name: agents_list
parameters.description: List of agent IDs (separated by comma), select a list of agents with size less or equal than 100

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Full path to the WPK file. The file must be on a folder on the Wazuh's installation directory (by default, /var/ossec)

parameters.name: installer
parameters.description: Installation script. Default is upgrade.sh or upgrade.bat for windows agents

parameters.description: Select which fields to return (separated by comma). Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

parameters.name: dhcp
parameters.description: Filter by network dhcp (enabled or disabled)

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query to filter results by. For example q="status=active"

get.description: Return the agent's OS info. This information include os information, architecture information among others of all agents

parameters.name: select
parameters.description: Select which fields to return (separated by comma). Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

get.description: Return the agent's packages info. This information include name, section, size, priority information of all packages among others

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: First element to return in the collection

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements to return. Although up to 100.000 can be specified, it is recommended not to exceed 500 elements. Responses may be slower the more this number is exceeded.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort the collection by a field or fields (separated by comma). Use +/- at the beggining to list in ascending or descending order. Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Look for elements containing the specified string. To obtain a complementary search, use '-' at the beggining

parameters.description: Select which fields to return (separated by comma). Use '.' for nested fields. For example, '{field1: field2}' may be selected with 'field1.field2'

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Filter by file format. For example 'deb' will output deb files

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query to filter results by. For example q="status=active"
